pi_items.source: Explicitly defined in the task description ('You are a floral designer...') and verifier comments.
Explicitly defined in the task description ('opening a new retail shop...') and verifier comments.
verifier.py assigns 5 sub-criteria scores of 20 points each, summing to a maximum of 100 points.
setup_task.sh explicitly generates this file as the task starting point by stripping furniture from SweetHome3DExample.sh3d.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a procedurally generated SweetHome3D starter file (floral_shop_starter.sh3d), which setup_task.sh creates by stripping all furniture from the official SweetHome3DExample.sh3d sample file. I discovered two critical defects in the evaluation logic. First, the task's export_result.sh script incorrectly attempts to read newly placed doors by checking for a 'doorOrWindow' attribute on '<pieceOfFurniture>' tags, but Sweet Home 3D actually saves doors using a separate '<doorOrWindow>' tag. Consequently, the verifier will always see 0 'new_doors' regardless of agent actions, making the door placement requirement of Criterion 1 impossible to pass. Second, because setup_task.sh only strips furniture, the starter shell begins with the 4 original named rooms ('terrasse', 'salon', 'cuisine', 'ch. 1') and 2 rooms with distinct floor colors intact. Since the verifier evaluates the total count of these items instead of the delta, the agent automatically receives the full 20 points for Criterion 2 (Room Zones & Flooring) without any action required. All metadata claims accurately reflect the stated design of the scripts, though the maximum achievable score is functionally capped at 90/100 due to the door counting bug.
